key_points: Number systems classify numbers into natural numbers, whole numbers, integers, rational numbers and irrational numbers
Rational numbers can be expressed in the form p/q where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0
Irrational numbers cannot be expressed as fractions and their decimal expansion is non-terminating and non-repeating
Real numbers include both rational and irrational numbers
Square roots of perfect squares are rational numbers
Square roots of non-perfect squares are irrational numbers
exam_tips: Check whether a number can be expressed as p/q
Identify perfect squares before simplifying square roots
Remember that rational numbers have terminating or repeating decimals
Practice identifying irrational numbers such as √2, √3 and π
